decisions.what: Created index.html with semantic HTML5 structure: header wrapping the hero section, main containing a social-proof placeholder section, and a footer with copyright and placeholder navigation links.
decisions.why: Specification required semantic HTML5 elements; the rule guide directs using <header>, <main>, <section>, <footer> instead of generic <div>s.

decisions.what: Placed the email form inside the hero <section> with <h1> ('Be the First to Know'), <h2> subheadline, an <input type='email'> with a properly associated <label for='email-input'>, a <button type='submit'>, and two hidden message containers (success and error) with aria-live='polite'.
decisions.why: Specification enumerated each of these elements and required label/input association plus aria-live regions for screen reader announcements.

decisions.what: Added the required JS-hook data-* attributes (data-waitlist-form, data-email-input, data-submit-button, data-success-message, data-error-message) and additional accessibility attributes (aria-label on the input and submit button, autocomplete='email').
decisions.why: Specification explicitly listed each data attribute and ARIA/autocomplete attribute for future interactivity and a11y.

decisions.what: Included SEO/meta tags in <head>: charset, viewport, title, description (150-160 char range), canonical, Open Graph tags (og:title, og:description, og:image with an Unsplash URL, og:url, og:type), Twitter Card tags, plus favicon links to favicon.ico and favicon.svg.
decisions.why: Specification required these meta tags and favicon links; Twitter Card tags were added because the rule guide's Tier 2 SEO section lists twitter:card as part of the standard set for public landing pages.

decisions.what: Added a skip link as the first child of <body> pointing to #main.
decisions.why: The rule guide mandates a skip link for full-page generation to support keyboard users.
